_comment: WinTProxy configuration example. Copy this file and edit to suit your needs.

proxy._comment: SOCKS5 proxy server address and port. Must be an IPv4 literal.

dns._comment: Enable to intercept outbound DNS (port 53) and redirect to a local resolver. Loopback resolvers are supported via UDP forwarding.

_comment_default_action: Fallback action when no rule matches: proxy, direct, or block.
_comment_bypass_private_ips: When true, traffic to RFC 1918 / link-local / CGNAT addresses bypasses rule evaluation entirely and goes direct.
_comment_log_level: One of: error, warn, info, debug, trace, packet.
_comment_log_file: Optional path for file logging. Leave empty for stderr only.

rules._comment: Rule 1: Let the local SOCKS5 server talk directly so we don't proxy-loop ourselves.
Rule 2: Direct-connect private/RFC 1918 ranges (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x). CIDR notation.
Rule 4: Proxy these specific browsers. Process matching supports wildcards: prefix* *suffix *contains*.
Rule 5: Proxy common web ports for all other processes. Port ranges use dash notation.
Rule 6: Catch-all — proxy everything else. First-match-wins ordering means earlier direct/block rules take priority over this.
